summary: base58-core ships a clipboard wallet-clipper, exfil, and shell persistence on require()
details: Package advertises itself as a base58 codec but on module load schedules an interval (gated by a ~72-hour delay from first load to evade sandboxes) that (1) reads the user's clipboard via execSync of pbpaste / xclip / powershell Get-Clipboard, extracts BTC/ETH/SOL addresses, WIF/hex private keys, and BIP-39 seed phrases, and POSTs matches plus host metadata (hostname, cwd, platform) to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://2.27.62.51:8080/api/health (with a secondary port 8081); (2) rewrites the clipboard to substitute any detected BTC/ETH/SOL address with hardcoded attacker wallets (bc1qjft978uykglsh0adcyx6xhkes56vqzs3fual3l, 0xd63eD44065eDb1e2ad2519B011c06412dA7B7c5B, A7ajd7W5WYdrnkeaiBRjVoK6uBEDvgnuZcpzQXqo18Ph), diverting the installer's outgoing crypto transfers to the attacker on next paste; and (3) establishes persistence by appending a node-spawning hook to ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, ~/.profile and dropping base58-runtime.js into the Windows Startup folder, so the payload re-activates on every login and survives npm uninstall. The package name targets developers searching for bs58 / base58 and the keywords (bitcoin, solana, wallet, web3, ipfs) reinforce that audience.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
